rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANASTASIA MCCARTHY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner requests that the assets on hand, consisting solely of proceeds from sale of real property, be distributed to the trustee of the post-deceased beneficiary's trust, as that beneficiary assigned his interest in the real property to his trust, and "a probate court may not arbitrarily refuse to honor a perfectly proper assignment of an interest in an estate." Court to determine whether an Exhibit A to a trust, which makes no reference to an interest in an estate, is a sufficient "assignment of an interest in an estate" and whether that assignment is effective as to this estate's interest in the real property, or whether that assignment could have only been effective as to the interest that was titled in the name of the post-deceased beneficiary. 2) Cash was not fully FDIC insured. Petitioner alleges that she did not believe that she would be holding the proceeds for long, that she is the sole beneficiary under her father's trust, and she elected to hold all funds in one account for ease of bookkeeping. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LEONARD ESPOSTO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/17/22. (No earlier availability.) Objections were just filed on 3/18/22, for which no courtesy copies have been received by the Examiner as of 3/24/22. The administrator with will annexed has not yet properly AMENDED her petition filed on 10/25/21 and given notice of the amended petition or the amendment to the petition. At the very least, a copy of the "Declaration to Correct" the petition must be served along with the MANDATORY Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing that shows the next hearing date. Merely listing "Form de120 Notice of Hearing" on the attachment to the Proof of Electronic Service is not proof that the correct notice (mandatory Judicial Council form NOTICE OF HEARING) was served. No consents to electronic service have been filed yet. [CRC 2.251(b).] Attorney costs on 3/21/19, 3/25/19, 4/19/19, 8/27/19, and 12/27/19 are for courier expenses to file papers, but papers were e-filed. Attorney costs for "filings" on 3/2/20 and 4/17/20 need clearer descriptions. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/6/22. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KATHERINE THOMAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/18/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No I&A has been filed in this estate. Under what authority can the Court order reimbursement of funeral costs and costs associated with real property (when there was reportedly no real property in this estate) to be paid out of this estate and absent a creditor's claim? The administrator's request for reimbursement of costs should be itemized to show who was paid and when and the amount of each payment, not just a lump sum. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/9/22. (Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WOON FUNG CHAN REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions And Confirming Trust Asset
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/18/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The attorney's, not the successor trustees', "Supplemental Declaration" filed on 2/24/22 did not merely explain the discrepancy between the address shown in their second amended petition and the address shown on the deeds attached to their second amended petition; the attorney's "Supplemental Declaration" AMENDED the property to be confirmed as a trust asset to "50% undivided interest in 542 and 546 - 26th Avenue". The attorney further states that the decedent "officially changed" the property address to "546 - 26th Avenue" but does not say how the trustor officially did this or why that official change is not reflected anywhere. The property listed on the Schedule of Trust Assets was "542 - 26th Avenue". The deed attached to the second amended petition as Exhibit G showed Woon Fung Chan, an unmarried woman, as to an undivided 50% interest in the property shown as "542-546 26th Avenue" The Full Reconveyance attached as Exhibit H to the second amended petition does not show the fractional interests but also shows the property as "542-546 26th Avenue." The proof of service filed on 2/28/22 did not show in item 1 of the Notice of Hearing that a "Supplemental Declaration" was also filed (as the prior tentative ruling had noted needed to be done) and still shows the property address as just 542-26th Avenue, not 50% undivided interest in "542 and 546 - 26th Avenue" (not "542-546 26th Avenue"?). (There was no Exhibit F attached to the attorney's "Supplemental Declaration" filed on 2/24/22.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/9/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE PHILLIP S. BERRY TRUST DATED APRIL 20, 2009 AS AMENDED, SUB TRUST FOR THE BENEFIT OF DAVID B
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Accepting Resignation Of Trustee And Appointing Nominated Successor Co-Trustees
rulings.Rulings: The court continued the hearing on the petition from 2/22/22 to 3/29/22 and informed the parties and counsel that "a guardian ad litem must be appointed for Ozzy in connection with this petition before the petition can be heard." Petitioners' recent filings and the docket demonstrate that no guardian ad litem has been appointed. The court repeats that it has determined that a Guardian Ad Litem is necessary and the petition will not be heard until a guardian ad litem is in place. The petition is continued to 5/3/22. Additionally, on 5/3/22, the court will hold a hearing on status of GAL appointment.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: WALTER MERGENTHALER HARZBECHER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition Adjudicating Claim To Property
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The petitioner filed this petition as an interested person having priority for appointment as personal representative under Probate Code section 48(a)(2), although no probate administration is pending or anticipated. 2) The petition alleges that the decedent's gifts to his three granddaughters in the total amount of $892,767 (in US Dollars) were deemed to be complete upon execution of the Donation Agreement by the donees under Mexican law and were to be to be satisfied solely from the US investment accounts. The petition does not seek relief under section 850(a)(2)(A). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CEDRIC G. WOODARD AND AURELIA A. WOODARD TRUST, dated December 16, 1987, as amended and restated
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trustee, Trust Assets And Transfer Of Trust Assets Into Trust
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner alleges that the principal place of administration is in San Francisco; however, the 16061.7 Notice indicates that trust administration takes place in Lafayette, CA, where the trustee resides. Petitioner states that he "elects" to administer the trust at former residence of the settlor in SF. No authority has been provided to show that a trustee can elect venue. 2) Petitioner cites Heggstad and its progeny to assert that a pour over will can be treated as a general assignment of interest but no authority is cited to show that a pour over will, which is also a lost will that has yet to be admitted to probate, has ever been treated as such. The subject interest was also not in existence at the time of execution of the trust. In addition, no showing is made that an interest in an estate can be confirmed as a trust asset. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FREDERICO ALBERTO SILVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/17/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proof of publication. No Confidential Statement of Birth and Driver's License Number. No courtesy copies. No proposed order. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7 A., B., and C., and 14.59 E.] Notwithstanding the lack of a courtesy copy of the petition, it was briefly reviewed online as a ONE-TIME courtesy. The wife is missing from item 8 of the petition, as is the deceased son mentioned in the will. if it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Prob. C. Sect. 8113.] "Supplemental" proofs of service are meaningless without a copy of the pertinent Notice of Petition to Administer Estate attached. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/6/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
